lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: I was up before dawn. Ran the wall twice.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: I ran it three times. But who's counting?

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: You are. You're always counting.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: I've been watching the squires stumble through morning drills for three days now. I have enough material for a comedy.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: You're writing about us?

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Someone has to document the decline of knighthood.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: You slept poorly. I can see it in your eyes.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Is it that obvious?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Like a willow after frost. Here — chew this root. It'll steady you.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Is this porridge or mortar?

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: In my chronicles, I'll record that the Knights of Solamnia were felled not by dragons, but by breakfast.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Eat it. You'll miss it on the road.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: In my family's hall, breakfast was served on silver. Four courses, minimum.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: And how'd that work out for your family?

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: ...I'll have the porridge, thank you.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Your footwork is sloppy. You're telegraphing every swing.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: At least I'm swinging. You spend more time posing than fighting.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: It's called form. You should try it sometime.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: They've been at it for an hour.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Riveting, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: I give them both a week before they're crying in the stables.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Don't lock your elbow. You'll break it.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: How do you know all this?

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Broke mine once. Learned the rest from watching others break theirs.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Did you see that disarm? I actually got him!

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: He let you. I saw his wrist turn.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Can't you just let me have one thing?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Your drills are clean. Orderly. The Dragon Army trained the same way.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: That's... not reassuring.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: It wasn't meant to be. Drill doesn't save you when the line breaks.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Est Sularus oth Mithas. My honor is my life. Do you ever wonder what that really means?

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: It means you'll die for a phrase you barely understand. Which is very Solamnic of you.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: The Oath of the Sword. My grandfather took it in this very hall.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: He said afterwards he felt nothing. Just the weight of it settling in.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: The Measure says a lot about duty. Not much about what happens after.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: That's because duty never ends, dear. Like weeding a garden.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: Historically, the Oath has been revised fourteen times. The current version omits a fascinating clause about poultry theft.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: ...poultry theft?

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: The Third Age was a complicated time.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Tell us a story, Thessaly. Something from the old wars.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Every story I know ends badly. That's what makes them worth telling.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Tell one that ends with everyone warm and fed. We could use it.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: You want to know what the Dragon Army campfires were like? Quieter than this. Nobody trusted anyone enough to talk.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: That sounds awful.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: It was efficient. I don't miss it.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Sera, you've been staring at the treeline for an hour.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Something moved out there.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: It's a rabbit. I saw it too. You can relax.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: I don't relax. I wait.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: I keep thinking about what comes next. After the training, after the Oath.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: More training. Then more after that. Get used to it.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: This castle has fascinating stonework. Pre-Cataclysm, I'd wager. See the jointing?

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: Your loss. It's magnificent.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: You look terrible.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Thanks. You look like you slept in your armor again.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: I did. It's called commitment.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: The dew was heavy this morning. Rain's coming. I can taste it.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: You can taste rain?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: The earth speaks to those who listen. Also, my knee aches.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Guard rotation is two minutes late. Sloppy.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: Perhaps they're just... enjoying the morning?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: In the Dragon Army, two minutes late meant two fewer fingers.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: ...I'll go check on them.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: I've been reviewing the castle records. The last Knight Commander here died of a bee sting.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: You're making that up.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: I never make things up. Reality is absurd enough.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: Lysander, do you ever wonder about the metaphysical implications of the Oath?

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: Fascinating. A man entirely unburdened by philosophy.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: I scouted the south ridge. Tracks. Boar, mostly. One set I couldn't identify.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Could be a displaced bear. They wander when the streams shift.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Could be. I set snares just in case.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Thessaly, what are you writing?

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Notes on your terrible posture during drills. Future scholars will find it illuminating.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: My posture is fine!

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: The notes disagree.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: In the old days, evenings at court involved music, wine, proper conversation—

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: And people starving outside the walls while you danced.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: ...That's fair. That's entirely fair.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Quiet night.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Quiet nights are the worst. Means something's waiting.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Or it means it's just quiet.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: You're an optimist. That's going to get you killed.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Last look at the castle. Strange — I couldn't wait to leave, and now...

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Nostalgia. It's a disease. You'll recover.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Finally. Open road. Real air.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: The castle had perfectly adequate air.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Castle air tastes like dust and regret.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: This road was a Dragon Army supply line. Two years ago, I marched it going the other direction.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Does that bother you?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Everything bothers me. I find it motivating.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: These mile markers are Pre-Cataclysm. Remarkable preservation.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: They're rocks.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: They're history carved in stone. Which is literally what rocks are for.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: We should decide quickly. Standing at crossroads gets people killed.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Metaphorically or literally?

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Both, in my experience.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: The wind is pushing south. When nature points, I listen.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: We're making tactical decisions based on wind now?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: The wind has outlived every general who ignored it.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Admit it — you'd be lost without me.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: I'd be quieter without you. That's not the same thing.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Close enough.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: I've been meaning to say — I know my name carries... complications. But I'm here to earn my place, not inherit one.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Then earn it. Words are free.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Commander, you've been scanning the horizon all day.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Old habit. The Dragon Army taught me what comes over horizons.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: And what's that?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Nothing good. But at least you see it coming.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: The trees here are sick. See how the bark peels? Something poisoned the water upstream.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Dragon Army?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: War. War poisons everything it touches. The earth remembers longer than we do.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: These charges are absurd. I healed people. Since when is that a crime?

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Since someone powerful needed a scapegoat. The law serves those who write it.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Then the law needs better authors.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: I know how these tribunals work. My family used them often — usually against people like Mira.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: And you're telling us this because...?

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: Because I know where the levers are. Let me help.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: We've come a long way from the training yard. I used to worry about holding a sword right. Now I worry about everything else.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: That's called growing up. It only gets worse.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Sera, I notice you've been less... prickly lately.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: I've been exactly the same.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: You shared your rations yesterday. Without being asked.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: I wasn't hungry. Don't read into it.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: You've held this group together longer than most commanders I served under. Don't let that go to your head.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: A compliment from Commander Serath. I should note the date — for historical purposes.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: It wasn't a compliment. It was a threat with context.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: You two have been walking closer together lately. I'm not judging — just observing.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Thessaly observes everything. It's her worst quality.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: My worst quality is my honesty. This is my second worst.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: First watch or second?

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: First. I don't trust you to stay awake.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: I stayed awake last time!

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: You were sleep-talking about cheese. That doesn't count.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: Did you know that campfire smoke repels certain spirits? Or attracts them. The sources disagree.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Comforting.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: Knowledge is always comforting. Even the terrifying kind.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Let me see those blisters. All of you. Don't think I haven't noticed the limping.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: It's nothing—

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Sit. Now. An untreated blister becomes an infection, and an infection becomes a grave. Sit.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: You served at Kalaman, didn't you?

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: South wall. Three months.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: I was on the other side. North approach.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Small world.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Getting smaller every day.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: I've been keeping a tally. Aldric and Vael have argued forty-three times since we left the castle.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Only forty-three?

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: I may have missed a few while sleeping. Though they argue in their sleep too, so it's hard to say.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: I used to have servants set up my camp. Lay out my bedroll. Warm my wine.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: And now?

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: Now I'm sleeping on roots and I've never been more awake. Funny how that works.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Love on the road is a fragile thing. Like a seedling in a storm. But sometimes that's when roots grow deepest.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Are you giving romantic advice through plant metaphors again?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Always.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Do you think we're ready? For whatever's coming?

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Nobody's ever ready. You just show up and do what needs doing.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: That's either very wise or very depressing.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: It's both. Welcome to soldiering.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: Thessaly, you've read the Ergothian Campaign Journals, yes?

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Cover to cover. Twice.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: Then you know we're approaching the exact terrain where Garibald's Third made their stand.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: They lost.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: Heroically, though. That counts for something.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: It does not.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: From here, there's no turning back. You all understand that.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: We understood that when we took the Oath.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Good. Just wanted to hear someone say it out loud.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: The land ahead feels... wrong. Like the earth is holding its breath.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: I feel it too. Wind's died. Birds stopped singing a mile back.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Stay close, all of you.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: If I don't make it through this — my journals are in the oilskin pouch. Left saddlebag. Promise me they'll reach the Library.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: You'll deliver them yourself.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Humor me. Chroniclers plan for the worst. It's professional.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Aldric. If things go badly in there — I want you to know you're not the worst squire I've trained with.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: That's the nicest thing you've ever said to me.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Don't get used to it.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Wolves don't attack groups this size. Not unless something's driving them.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Agreed. Something worse is out there, and we just met its dinner.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Those wolves were starving. Ribs showing through the fur. Whatever's poisoning this land is killing everything.

lines.speaker: Tariel
lines.text: A corruption this deep suggests magical origin. Dark magic, sustained over months.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Then we find the source and end it. The land deserves that much.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: My hands are shaking. Is that normal?

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Means you're still alive. When they stop shaking is when you worry.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: How are yours?

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Steady. But I've had more practice being terrified.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: This is it, then. The moment where we find out what we're actually made of.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Bones and bad decisions, mostly.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: ...I was going for inspirational.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: I know. It was a good effort.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: A duel. Proper, formal, by the Measure. This is how it should be decided.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: You sound almost excited.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: I respect the form. Even when the stakes are terrible, the form matters.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: In the Dragon Army, disputes were settled by the commander's word. No appeal. No ceremony.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: And here?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Here you get to bleed for the privilege of being heard. I suppose that's progress.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: The smoke — I can smell it from here. That's not just wood burning. That's homes. Lives.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Then we move faster.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Yes. We do.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: I've seen villages burn before. From the other side. I told myself it was necessary.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: And now?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Now I know better. That's the punishment — knowing.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: I'll record every name. Every face I can remember. They deserve that.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: They deserve more than words on a page.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: They do. But words are what I have. And words outlast everything — even this.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: A tavern fight? Really? I thought Fighters had more dignity than this.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Dignity is for people who haven't been in enough taverns.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Your grip's wrong for a bar fight. Choke up on the handle — less reach, more control in tight spaces.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Since when are you an expert on tavern brawling?

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Everyone has hobbies.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Mercenaries. I know how they think — loyalty to coin, not cause. Watch for the ones who won't meet your eyes. They're the dangerous ones.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Speaks from experience?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: I was the one who wouldn't meet anyone's eyes. Draw your own conclusions.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: First night on the march. Strange to think we may never see the Spur again.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: You say that like it's a bad thing.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: It's the only home I've known for three years.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Then build a better one. That's what stories are for.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: The stars are different here. Clearer. No torch smoke.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: You watch the stars?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: My grandmother taught me. She said they're the eyes of the gods. Watching to see what we'll do next.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: The enemy chose this ground. That means they had time to prepare.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Traps?

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Worse. Confidence. A trap you can disarm. Confidence you have to break.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: I crossed this river once before. Going the other way. Wearing different colors.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: And now?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Now I cross it going the right direction. That has to count for something.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: The Vingaard Ford. Three thousand years of history. Huma himself crossed here.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Did he complain about his boots getting wet?

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: The chronicles are silent on the matter. I choose to believe yes.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: In the Dragon Army, mornings meant roll call. Missing it meant punishment. Missing it twice meant death.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: And here?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Here someone asks if I slept well. It's unsettling in the best possible way.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: The great hall. Empty now. But these stones remember feasts, coronations, oaths sworn in firelight.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: And now they'll remember us.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: That's the weight of it, isn't it? We become part of the story whether we're ready or not.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: My family had maps of this road. Trade routes, supply lines, safe harbors. All worthless now.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Maps don't show what matters.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: No. They don't show the people. That's what I'm learning.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: There are herbs growing wild here. Useful ones. In the city, they'd cost a week's wages.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Should we stop?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: No. But I'm noting the location. For after. There has to be an after.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: An ambush. Someone's been watching the road.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Dragon Army scouts. They've been tracking us for two days.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: You knew and didn't say anything?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: I was waiting to see if you'd notice. You did. Good.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Survivors. Good. That means there's something left to save.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: You sound almost optimistic.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: Don't get used to it. It's a temporary condition.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: I've seen this before. Villages burned to force refugees south. It's a tactic.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: A tactic.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Don't look at me like that. Naming it doesn't mean I approve. It means I understand. Those are different things.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: The courtyard's quiet. Too quiet.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: That's because everyone's waiting to see what you'll do.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Wonderful. No pressure at all.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: I trained here for two years. Every stone has a memory.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Good memories?

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Useful ones. That's better.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: A correspondent from the Library. I should be taking notes.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: You're always taking notes.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: And you're always giving me material. It's a partnership.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: News from the south. The rivers are running strange colors, they say.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: What does that mean?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Nothing good. The land speaks, if you know how to listen.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: This tent smells of yarrow and feverfew. Good signs. The healer knows their craft.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: You can tell that from the smell?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: I can tell many things from smells. For instance, you haven't washed your feet in three days.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: ...Fair point.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Wounded soldiers. Broken weapons. This is what war looks like before the chroniclers clean it up.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: I don't clean anything up.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Then you're a rare one. Keep it that way.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Becklin's plan is sound. I've studied the terrain — the ambush point is perfect.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: You sound almost like an officer.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Don't remind me. The responsibility is terrifying.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Good. Terror keeps you sharp.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: This ambush plan — it would work on Dragon Army regulars. But not Highlord troops.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: You don't think they're sending their best?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Not yet. But when they do, we'll know.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Ambush. Classic pincer formation. Someone read the same manuals we did.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: That's not comforting.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: It's not meant to be. It's meant to keep you alive.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: They've cut off our retreat. Clever.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Clever? We're trapped!

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Which means we can't retreat. Only advance. Sometimes being trapped is a gift.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Hold still. This wound needs stitching, not heroics.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: I can manage—

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: You can manage to be quiet while I work. That's what you can manage.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: Blood on my cloak. My mother would be horrified.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Your mother isn't here.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: No. She isn't. And I find I don't mind as much as I expected.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Refugees. Families. Children carrying what's left of their lives.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: We have to help them.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: We have to win. That's how we help them. Everything else is a kindness we can't afford.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: That's cold.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: It's true. Those are often the same thing.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: I did this. Not personally, but — the Dragon Army did this. I was part of it.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: And now you're on the other side.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: Am I? Some days I'm not sure there are sides. Just people trying to survive what other people did.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: The column's slowing. Exhaustion or fear — both, probably.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Should I say something? Give a speech?

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: No. Walk among them. Let them see you're tired too. That's worth more than words.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: You're full of wisdom today.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: I read a lot. It rubs off.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: The scouts report clear skies ahead. For once, no ambush.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: That's suspicious.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: I know. But I'll take suspicious over certain death.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: Every kingdom falls. It's just a matter of how and when and who remembers.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: That's grim.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: That's history. Grim is just the honest word for it.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: The herbs here are different. Stronger. The soil must be richer.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: You notice everything, don't you?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: I notice what keeps people alive. Everything else is decoration.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: Your footwork's improved. Still sloppy on the pivot, but improved.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Did you just compliment me?

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: I stated a fact. Don't get emotional about it.

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: I know this road. The Dragon Army used it for supply lines.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Anything we should know?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: There's a cache buried two miles east. Food, weapons, gold. If it hasn't been found.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: And you're just mentioning this now?

lines.speaker: Serath
lines.text: I wasn't sure I wanted to share it. Now I am.

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: My family's banner hung in this hall once. I remember the feasts.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Caeron
lines.text: Politics. Money. The usual poisons. We lost everything, and I learned that names mean nothing without the strength to back them.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Something's wrong with the water here. I can taste it.

lines.speaker: Sera
lines.text: Don't drink then.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: I wasn't planning to. But the others don't know. Warn them.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: We've been moving too fast. The column will break apart at this pace.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: We don't have a choice.

lines.speaker: Vael
lines.text: There's always a choice. Sometimes all the options are just terrible.

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: You've changed since we left the Spur. Grown, I think.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Is that your way of saying I'm doing well?

lines.speaker: Lysander
lines.text: It's my way of saying you're not dead yet. That's the best compliment a soldier gives.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: The scribes of Palanthas will never believe this. I barely believe it myself.

lines.speaker: Aldric
lines.text: Then write it down.

lines.speaker: Thessaly
lines.text: I am. Every word. Every death. The truth deserves that much.
